Final result: Spondylolisthesis.

current_turn_information: Document 1: Common physical examination findings of cauda equina syndrome include hypesthesia, saddle dysesthesia, Lasegue sign, decreased knee and ankle reflex bilaterally, asymmetric calf wasting, lower limb weakness, positive Babinski sign, impaired proprioception.
Document 2: Cauda equina syndrome must be differentiated from spinal disc herniation, epidural hematoma, spinal tumor/metastasis, spinal stenosis, and diabetic amyotrophy.

Document 3: If left untreated, 100% progress to permanent nerve damage and neurological deficit. Common complications include micturition dysfunction 48%, defecation dysfunction 42%, sexual dysfunction 53%, sciatica 48%, altered sensation.
Document 4: Cauda equina syndrome is due to compression of the cauda equina in the lumbar region. The cauda equina consists of spinal nerves L2-L5, S1-S5, and the coccygeal nerve.
Document 5: Medical Treatment: 5.4 mg/kg·h of methylprednisolone (IV) for 2 days, 5 mg of dexamethasone (IV) every 12 h.

case_memory.prior_information_summary: 45-year-old female with longstanding L4-L5 listhesis, asymptomatic until recent fall in park with pop in back, causing spontaneous onset of back pain, burning pain radiating to left foot, left foot weakness and foot drop. History of urinary retention, evaluated by urologist with cystoscopy showing no bladder/urethral abnormalities. Bilateral L4 spondylosis with severe central stenosis at L4-L5. Recent unintentional voiding of urine. Bowel: constipation only. Pain rated at rest and with activity, exacerbated by walking, prolonged standing, lying on side. Physical exam: left foot drop, cannot stand on heels on left, forward flexed posture, normal spinal alignment, bilateral lower extremity weakness except left tibialis anterior, absent patellar and Achilles reflexes, no clonus, Babinski downgoing, full hip/knee motion, negative straight leg raise. Past medical history: asthma, COPD, colonic diverticulitis with colectomy, gastric ulcer, DVT with Coumadin/IVC filter, bipolar disorder, PTSD. Final result: Spondylolisthesis.
